analyst.opinion: Anxiety

narrative.why: Score 39 sits squarely in the Anxiety band (39-46); the market is nervous and uncertain, not yet in full panic but far from complacent, and the low F&G of 25 confirms the fear is real.

judge.why: Score 39 sits in the Anxiety band (39-46) and the Fear&Greed at 25 with a 0.79 bottom-match says we've climbed one rung off the pain floor into nervous chop — not yet Disbelief, no longer Panic.

analyst.opinion: Complacency
analyst.why: Bottom analog from Aug 2024 suggests the flush is done, F&G 25 is stabilizing not collapsing, and score 39 is at the Anxiety floor — leg is rising, next stop is the calm drift of Complacency as recovery tentatively builds.

narrative.why: The path of least resistance is down—score 39 is at the very bottom of Anxiety, one bad flush from slipping into Panic (31-38), and the heavy fear reading suggests sellers are still in control.

judge.why: If the bounce stalls here without fresh catalysts, the crowd exhausts its nervous energy and drifts into low-conviction calm one rung up; front-running the turn down from here is premature given the strong bottom analog.

analyst.opinion: medium

narrative.why: Score is pinned at the Anxiety floor; a break lower into Panic is the modal outcome, but chop within Anxiety is also possible if fear doesn't accelerate into active selling.

judge.why: The 0.79 August-bottom match argues for a continued rise toward Disbelief, but F&G 25 and the score sitting at the Anxiety floor mean a slip back to Denial is equally plausible — evidence is genuinely split.

analyst.opinion: BULL

narrative.why: Score 39 in Anxiety with F&G at 25 and a capitulation-like pattern match—this is a fear-driven market where rallies are sold, not bought.

judge.opinion: The market just crawled out of the max-pain cellar and is blinking in the daylight, unsure whether it's safe — the August 2024 playbook says it is, and the fear gauge at 25 is the crowd telling you they don't believe it yet. That's the edge.
